name: Beef Pot Roast, Oriental Style
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Ginger, fresh grated (or 1/4 teaspoon ground ginger)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven or large pan with a tight-fitting cover, brown the meat in fat. Season with salt and pepper, then remove from the pan. Pour off the fat drippings.
2. Add onion, green pepper, soy sauce, wine, ginger, water, and mushrooms; mix well.
3. Return the meat to the pan. Cover and simmer for 3½ to 4 hours, or until done. Alternatively, cook in a 325°F oven for the same amount of time.
4. Turn the meat once to ensure even cooking throughout. When done, remove the meat and keep warm.
5. For 2 cups of gravy, pour the liquid from the pan into a 2-cup measuring cup. Let stand for 1 minute to allow fat to rise to the top. Discard all but 4 tablespoons (or less) of fat. Add enough water (or other liquid) to measure 1½ cups of liquid. Return to the pan.
6. In the same cup, mix 1/2 cup cold water with cornstarch. Add this mixture slowly to the liquid in the pan while stirring constantly. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 3 minutes. Taste the gravy and adjust seasoning if necessary with salt and pepper.
7. Slice the meat; serve with sauce over hot rice.
